title: Subtract decimals (hundredths)
description: Subtract small whole numbers, tenths, and hundredths like 0.6-0.43 or 1.58-0.5

hints: There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two ways we can solve it.

###Place Value

We can think in terms of hundredths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}1.6 - 1.31\\\\
&=1.60 - 1.31\\\\
&=160\text{ hundredths} - 131\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=29\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=0.29
\end{align}$

###Place value table

Let's line up the numbers by their decimal places. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
- | - | - | - | -
 $\blueE{1}$ | $.$ | $\blueE{6}$ | $\blueE0$
$\maroonD1$ | $.$ | $\maroonD{3}$ | $\maroonD1$

Now, let's subtract each place value column.

| Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
- | - | - | - | -
 | $\blueE{1}$ | $.$ | $\overset{5}{\cancel{\blueE{6}}}$ | $\overset{10}{\cancel{\blueE{0}}}$
$-$  | $\maroonD1$ | $.$ | $\maroonD{3}$ | $\maroonD1$
 $\underline{~~~~}$ | $\underline{~~~~}$|  | $\underline{~~~~}$| $\underline{~~~~}$
 | $0$ | $.$| $2$ | $9$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two ways we can solve it.

&=371\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=3.71
\end{align}$
